original_title: HH072-25-HB Specialist Environmental Aids for Deaf People

english_summary: NHS Highland is seeking a qualified organisation to provide a comprehensive service for specialist environmental aids and equipment for individuals with hearing loss across the Highland region. eligibility_requirements: Suitably qualified organisation (specific certifications, experience requirements, or company size requirements not detailed in the notice).

buyer_name: The Common Services Agency (more commonly known as NHS National Services Scotland) (NSS)
english_summary: This is a framework tender by NHS National Services Scotland for the provision of comprehensive cardiology catheterization laboratory equipment and consumables, including various types of catheters, stents, guidewires, imaging consumables, and associated accessories. The framework covers 20 scored lots of specific cardiology products plus additional unscored lots for accessories, innovation items, and commitment arrangements.
what_they_want: Balloon Catheters, Diagnostic Catheters, Guide Catheters, Guide Extension Catheters, Femoral Introducers, Radial Introducers, Radial Compression Devices, FFR Measurement Systems, Intra Aortic Balloon Catheters, Inflation Devices, Vascular Closure Devices, IVUS Consumables, OCT Consumables, Workhorse Drug Eluting Stents, High Risk Bleed Drug Eluting Stents, Niche Drug Eluting Stents, Drug Eluting Balloons, Angio Guidewires, Calcium Management products, and associated accessories for cardiology procedures.
evaluation_criteria: The framework includes 20 scored lots and several unscored lots (Accessories Lot, Innovation Lot, Commitment Lot, and Calcium Management Lot). Suppliers can only be awarded unscored accessory and commitment lots if they have been successfully awarded to one or more of the scored lots 1-20. Specific scoring criteria and weightings are not provided in this notice.

buyer_name: APUC Limited
english_summary: This is a framework tender for the supply of microscopy and imaging equipment for Scottish educational and research institutions. The procurement covers a wide range of optical microscopes from low-end teaching equipment (under £10,000) to high-end research systems (over £10,000), including advanced laser microscopes, imaging components, light sources, and high-content screening systems.
what_they_want: Low-end optical microscopes (under £10,000 excl. VAT) including compound microscopes (upright/inverted with optional fluorescence and cameras), stereo/zoom microscopes, and gel documentation systems; High-end optical microscopes (over £10,000 excl. VAT) including compound microscopes, stereo/zoom microscopes, label-free holographic microscopes, automated live cell microscopes, and slide scanners; Advanced laser microscopy systems including confocal, spinning disc, TIRF, light sheet, and multi-photon microscopes; Super resolution microscopy equipment (STED, GSD, SPDMphymod); FLIM/FRET systems and structured illumination systems; Camera and detector components (specialist cameras, photo multiplier tubes, FLIM detectors); Light sources (incandescent lamps, halogen bulbs, arc lamps, LED light sources, lasers); High-content screening systems with associated software and automatic sample handling equipment.
eligibility_requirements: Economic operators may be excluded if they fall under any situations referred to in regulation 58 of the Public Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (relating to grounds for mandatory and discretionary exclusion such as criminal convictions, tax/social security payment defaults, insolvency, professional misconduct).
